name: Grilled Fish w/ Garlic Beurre Rouge
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup olive oil, green fruity variety
6 pieces grouper, or other firm fish
2 small green chili peppers, finely minced
recipeInstructions:
1. In a bowl, combine olive oil, 5 tablespoons lemon juice, slivered garlic, 1/2 cup chopped cilantro, salt, and pepper. Mix well.
2. Add fish fillets to the marinade and marinate for at least one hour or up to overnight in the refrigerator.
3. To prepare garlic beurre rouge: In a frying pan over medium heat, melt 4 tablespoons butter. Stir in chopped onion, chili peppers, and minced garlic until softened. Cook for about 5 minutes, stirring frequently.
4. Add tomatoes and remaining lemon juice to the pan. Simmer for another 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
5. Remove from heat and season with salt and pepper as needed. Stir in the remaining chopped cilantro.
6. Slowly stir in the remaining butter until melted.
7. Drain any excess marinade from the fish fillets.
8. Grill or broil the fillets over low coals for about 4-5 minutes on each side, turning only once. Be careful not to overcook.
9. Transfer the grilled fillets to a warm serving platter and top with garlic beurre rouge. Garnish with lemon slices and cilantro sprigs before serving.
